name: Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc.
business: Ultragenyx Pharmaceutical Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing therapies for rare and ultra-rare genetic diseases. The company has four approved products: Crysvita for X-Linked Hypophosphatemia and Tumor-Induced Osteomalacia, Mepsevii for Mucopolysaccharidosis VII, Dojolvi for Long-chain Fatty Acid Oxidation Disorders, and Evkeeza for Homozygous Familial Hypercholesterolemia. It also has several clinical-stage product candidates in gene therapy, biologic, small molecule, and nucleic acid categories.

exposures.sentence: We are a biopharmaceutical company committed to bringing novel products to patients for the treatment of serious rare and ultra-rare genetic diseases.

catalysts.event: Resubmission of BLA for UX111 seeking accelerated approval for Sanfilippo syndrome type A
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we resubmitted our BLA seeking accelerated approval for UX111 (rebisufligene etisparvovec) as a treatment for patients with Sanfilippo syndrome Type A (MPS IIIA) to the FDA.

catalysts.event: PDUFA action date for DTX401 for Glycogen Storage Disease Type Ia
catalysts.sentence: The company expects a PDUFA action date in the third quarter 2026, based on FDA regulations.

catalysts.event: Data from Phase 3 Aspire study of GTX-102 for Angelman syndrome expected
catalysts.sentence: Data from this study are expected in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Data from Phase 2/3 Aurora study of GTX-102 expected
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, we announced enrollment had begun in the Phase 2/3 Aurora study, which evaluates GTX-102 in other Angelman syndrome genotypes and ages.

catalysts.event: Data from Stage 1 of Cyprus2+ study of UX701 for Wilson disease expected
catalysts.sentence: Data from Stage 1 of this study are expected in 2026.

catalysts.event: Received Incomplete Response Letter from FDA for UX111 BLA resubmission
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, we received an Incomplete Response Letter, or IRL, from the FDA regarding our resubmitted BLA.

catalysts.event: Completion of enrollment in Phase 3 Enh3ence study of DTX301 for OTC deficiency
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we announced enrollment had been completed in the Phase 3 Enh3ence study of DTX301 for the treatment of OTC deficiency with a total of 37 patients randomized 1:1 to DTX301 or placebo.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 48,000 patients with XLH in the developed world, including approximately 36,000 adults and 12,000 children.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 48,000 patients with XLH in the developed world, including approximately 36,000 adults and 12,000 children.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 2,000 to 4,000 patients with TIO in the developed world.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 2,000 to 4,000 patients with TIO in the developed world.

tamClaims.claim: MPS VII is one of the rarest MPS disorders, affecting an estimated 200 patients in the developed world.
tamClaims.sentence: MPS VII is one of the rarest MPS disorders, affecting an estimated 200 patients in the developed world.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 8,000 to 14,000 patients in the developed world with LC-FAOD.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 8,000 to 14,000 patients in the developed world with LC-FAOD.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 3,000 to 5,000 patients with HoFH in the developed world outside of the U.S.
tamClaims.sentence: There are approximately 3,000 to 5,000 patients with HoFH in the developed world outside of the U.S.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 3,000 to 5,000 patients in the developed world affected by Sanfilippo syndrome type A.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 3,000 to 5,000 patients in the developed world affected by Sanfilippo syndrome type A.

tamClaims.claim: GSDIa is the most common genetically inherited glycogen storage disease, with an estimated 6,000 patients in the developed world.
tamClaims.sentence: DTX401 is being developed for the treatment of patients with GSDIa, and is the most common genetically inherited glycogen storage disease, with an estimated 6,000 patients in the developed world.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 60,000 patients in the developed world affected by Angelman syndrome.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 60,000 patients in the developed world affected by Angelman syndrome.

tamClaims.claim: There are approximately 10,000 patients in the developed world with OTC deficiency, of which we estimate approximately 80% are classified as late-onset.
tamClaims.sentence: DTX301 is being developed for the treatment of patients with OTC deficiency, which is the most common urea cycle disorder, and there are approximately 10,000 patients in the developed world with OTC deficiency, of which we estimate approximately 80% are classified as late-onset, our target population.

tamClaims.claim: Wilson disease affects approximately 50,000 patients in the developed world.
tamClaims.sentence: UX701 is being developed for the treatment of patients with Wilson disease, which affects approximately 50,000 patients in the developed world.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 60,000 patients in the developed world affected by OI.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 60,000 patients in the developed world affected by OI.
